1.title: Display Rules & Action Page Without Digital Twin Data

content.text: The system shall display the Rules and Action page under RAMS → Rack → IRDS → Rules and Action even when no Digital Twin data is available.
access the Rules & Action page even when no Digital Twin exists
I can understand the setup requirements.
the Digital Twin has no element data,
I navigate to RAMS → Rack → IRDS → Rules and Action,
the Rules & Action page shall load normally (not blocked or hidden).

2.title: Empty State When No Digital Twin Elements Exist

content.text: The system shall detect the absence of Digital Twin elements and show an Empty State with the message “No Elements in Digital Twin Yet.”
an Empty State message when there are no Digital Twin elements
I understand why no elements appear.
the Digital Twin element list is empty,
the Rules and Action page loads,
the system shows an empty state:

3.title: Informational Text for Missing Digital Twin Elements

content.text: The system shall display an informational text stating that “Your Digital Twin doesn’t have any mapped elements yet. Once elements are created in the Digital Twin module, they’ll automatically appear here for rule configuration.
an explanation message about missing Digital Twin mapping
I know what must be done next.
“Your Digital Twin doesn’t have any mapped elements yet. Once elements are created in the Digital Twin module, they’ll automatically appear here for rule configuration.”
The system shall display a button labelled “Go to Digital Twin” that navigates the user to the Digital Twin module.
I can directly navigate to the Digital Twin module to add elements.
I click the “Go to Digital Twin” button,
the system navigates to the Digital Twin module.
The system shall auto-sync the Rules table when Digital Twin data is added.
the Rules table to auto-sync when new Digital Twin data is added
I always see the latest elements without manual refresh.
new elements are added in Digital Twin,
I return to or refresh the Rules & Action page,
the table updates automatically with new elements.
The system shall display a searchable and sortable Rules & Action table containing: Element ID, Element Name, Category, Status, Active/Inactive Toggle, and Action Menu.
a full table listing elements for rule configuration
I can manage their rules and actions.
The system shall include a Search Bar at the top to filter elements dynamically as text is typed.
I can quickly find the element I need.
the table filters dynamically based on Element ID or Name.
The System shall assign a default category as “structural” for the elements added from Digital Twin, and
When the user click add new element or adds element manually the system shall assign “general category to that element.
a new element is imported from Digital Twin,
the system assigns Category = Structural by default.
I add a new element manually (Add New Element),
the system assigns Category = General by default.
The system shall allow each element’s Status to be toggled between Active and Inactive using a switch button.
I can control its visibility and rule usage.
the element’s status updates accordingly in real-time.
The system shall display the current status in text format beside the toggle: “Active” (blue) or “Inactive” (grey).
see the current status in text form
I immediately understand the element’s active state.
display text next to the toggle:
The system shall allow users to perform additional actions for each element via the three-dot (⋮) menu in the Action column:
an action menu for each element
I click the (⋮) menu for any element,

12.title: Show Confirm Deactivation Popup When Toggling Element Off

content.text: When the user toggles an element from Active → Inactive (ON->OFF) the system shall open a “Confirm Deactivation” popup. with the message text “You’re about to deactivate this element. All associated activity phases and observations will also become unavailable in inspections. Please confirm this action.”
a confirmation popup to appear when I toggle an element from Active → Inactive
I do not accidentally disable the element and its related inspection data.
the user toggles it to Inactive (OFF)
the system shall open a “Confirm Deactivation” popup
the popup shall display the message:
“You’re about to deactivate this element. All associated activity phases and observations will also become unavailable in inspections. Please confirm this action.”

13.title: Display Buttons in Confirm Deactivation Popup

content.text: The confirmation Deactivation pop-up shall display 2 buttons Cancel and Confirm & Deactivate.
the deactivation confirmation popup to provide Cancel and Confirm options
I can either stop or proceed with the deactivation action.

14.title: Deactivate Element and Disable Linked Data

content.text: Clicking Confirm & Deactivate shall: Deactivate the element, Disable its associated phases and observation types.
confirming deactivation to disable the element and all its associated phases/observation types
the system accurately removes all dependent inspection options.
Clicking Confirm & Deactivate shall display a success toast “Element status updated successfully”.
I know the element status was updated successfully.
a success toast shall appear with the message:

16.title: Cancel Reverts Toggle to Original State

content.text: Clicking Cancel shall close the modal and revert the toggle back to its original (Active) state. In the rules and actions table list. status column.
clicking Cancel to close the popup and revert the toggle
the toggle in the Rules & Actions table shall revert to its original Active (ON) state in the Status column.

17.title: Show Confirm Activation Popup When Toggling OFF → ON

content.text: When the user toggles an element from Inactive → Active, (OFF-> ON) the system shall open a “Confirm Activation” pop-up, with the message – “You’re about to activate this element. All associated activity phases and observations will be available for inspectors during issue reporting.”
a confirmation popup to appear when I toggle an element from Inactive → Active
I do not accidentally enable inspection elements and their associated data.
the user toggles it to Active (ON)
the system shall open a “Confirm Activation” popup
the popup shall display the message:
“You’re about to activate this element. All associated activity phases and observations will be available for inspectors during issue reporting.”

18.title: Buttons Displayed in Confirm Activation Popup

content.text: The Confirm Activation pop-up shall display 2 buttons Cancel and Confirm & Activate.
the Confirm Activation popup to provide Cancel and Confirm options
I can either proceed or stop the activation action.
the popup shall show two buttons:

19.title: Activation Enables Element and Associated Data

content.text: Clicking Confirm & Activate shall: Activate the element and make its associated phases and observation types available.
clicking Confirm & Activate to enable the element and all associated activity phases and observation types
inspectors can use them during issue reporting.
Clicking Confirm & Activate shall display a success toast “Element status updated successfully”.
I know the action was completed successfully.
the element has been successfully activated
a success toast shall appear with the message:

21.title: Cancel Reverts Toggle to Original Inactive State

content.text: Clicking Cancel shall close the modal and revert the toggle back to its original (Inactive) state. In the rules and actions table list status column.
clicking Cancel to close the activation modal and revert the toggle
the toggle in the Rules & Actions table shall revert to its original Inactive (OFF) state.

22.title: Status Column Updates Immediately After State Change

content.text: After successful activation or deactivation, the system shall update the Status column immediately.
the Status column to update immediately when an element is activated or deactivated
I always see the correct/current state without refreshing the page.
an activation or deactivation action is completed
the Status column shall refresh instantly to show the current state (Active or Inactive).
The system shall show pagination controls at the bottom of the table allowing navigation between pages and adjusting rows per page (e.g., 10,25,50).
I can navigate large lists of elements efficiently.
pagination appears at the bottom of the table
I can select rows per page: 10, 25, 50
On clicking Edit Activity Phase, the system shall navigate to the Activity Phase management page for the selected element.
open the Activity Phase management page for an element
I can manage that element’s phases.
I am on the Rules & Action table,
I click Edit Activity Phase from the action menu (⋮),
the system navigates to the Activity Phase management page

25.title: Empty State When No Activity Phases Exist

content.text: If no phases exist, the system shall display the message “No Activity Phases Yet.”
The system shall display an informational message “You haven’t added or imported any activity phases for this element.
I know no phases have been added yet.
the selected element has zero phases,
“You haven’t added or imported any activity phases for this element.”
The system shall display a “+ Add Activity Phase” button for creating new phases.
see a button to add new phases
I can begin creating activity phases.
I am on the Activity Phase page,
a “+ Add Activity Phase” button is visible.
On clicking Add Activity Phase, the system shall open a new screen to capture phase details.
open a new screen for entering activity phase details
the system opens a new screen to capture phase details.
The system shall display an “Import from Library” button for importing predefined phase templates.
an option to import predefined phase templates
I don’t need to recreate them manually.
I am on the Activity Phase page,
an “Import from Library” button is displayed.
The System shall display a modal at left side that contains library list to select library from that list.
the Import Library modal to appear on the left side
I can easily browse available libraries.
a modal opens on the left side
Containing the list of available libraries.
Based on the selected source, the system shall load and display the list of libraries on the left side.
libraries to be loaded based on the selected source
I see the correct library list.
the Import Library modal is open,
the left panel loads the corresponding libraries.
Libraries shall be displayed as radio-list cards (one library selectable at a time).
libraries to be displayed as radio-style cards
I can choose only one library at a time.
the Import Library modal is visible,
only one library can be selected at a time.
Until a library is selected, the preview panel on the right must show the message: “Select a library to preview.”
an instructional message before selecting a library
I understand what to do next.
The modal shall contain Cancel and Next buttons at the bottom right.
Cancel and Next buttons in the modal
I can close or continue the import process.
Cancel and Next buttons appear at the bottom-right.

34.title: Disable Next Button Until Library Selected

content.text: The Next button must remain disabled until a library is selected.
Next to remain disabled until a library is chosen
I cannot move forward without a selection.
Clicking Cancel shall close the modal without applying any changes.
I can exit without importing anything.

36.title: Load Clause Preview on Library Selection

content.text: When the user selects a library card, the preview panel (right side) must load clause details for that library.
preview clause details of the selected library
I can confirm it is the correct choice.
the preview panel to show clause information
The preview panel shall include a “View All Clauses” button.
I can view the complete clause list.
Clicking “View All Clauses” shall open the complete clause list in a popup modal.
open a popup showing the full clause list
I can review all clauses clearly.
a modal opens displaying all clauses for the selected library.
The View All Clauses modal shall appear centered above the Import Library modal.
the clause list modal centered above the Import Library modal
it appears prominent and easy to read.
the user clicks View All Clauses,
the “All Clauses” popup appears centered on screen

41.title: View All Clauses Modal Title & Subtitle

content.text: The popup shall include title: “All Clauses” and subtitle showing the library name, standard name and number of clauses for e.g., “RAMS-Europe (EN-15635) 6 clauses”.
see the library name, standard name, and clause count
I understand which library is being previewed.
The popup shall list all clauses in a table with columns:
I can see the full structure at once.
Clauses shall be fully scrollable within the modal.
clauses inside the modal to be scrollable
I can view long lists without the modal overflowing.
the clause list area supports vertical scrolling
A Close button shall be available at the bottom right of the popup and
Clicking Close return the user to the Import Library modal without losing selection.
a Close button in the popup
I can exit the popup without losing my library selection.
a Close button appears at the bottom-right.
the Import Library modal remains open
my previously selected library remains selected.

45.title: Proceed to Next Step After Library Selection

content.text: Clicking Next after selecting a library shall navigate to the next step of the import process.
the Next button to take me to the next step
I can continue the import workflow.
the system navigates to the next import step.
On clicking next the system shall redirect the user to step 2 “Choose Elements”.
clicking Next to bring me to Step 2
I can choose which elements to apply the imported library to.
the system loads Step 2: Choose Elements.
The stepper shall display Step 1 as completed (blue tick).
The stepper shall highlight Step 2 as the active step in bold/blue.
Step 3 shall be visible but disabled until 2 is completed.
the stepper to show correct progression
I understand where I am in the process.
Step 3 → Visible but disabled until Step 2 is completed.
Step 2 shall display the section title: “Choose Elements”,
text must appear below it: “Select the elements where you want to apply the imported library.”
a clear section title and instruction
Text: “Select the elements where you want to apply the imported library.”
The system shall display all elements from the selected library in a two-column format:
elements displayed in a Warehouse vs Library comparison
elements appear in a two-column format:

50.title: Elements List Must Include Standard Warehouse Components

content.text: Baseplate, Upright, Beam, Bracing, Units topper, Run spacer, Tie-beam, Pallet, Safety Accessory, Connector Plate.
see all standard warehouse elements in the list
I can apply the imported library to them.
The table shall support vertical scrolling when elements exceed the visible area.
the elements list is navigable for long lists.
the element list section supports vertical scrolling.
Each row shall contain a checkbox on the left for selection.
each element row contains a checkbox on the left.
Clicking a row or checkbox must highlight the row with a light blue background to indicate active selection.
I clearly see which elements are selected.
I click a checkbox or element row,
the row is highlighted in light blue
User shall be able to select any combination of elements.
the flexibility to select any number of elements
I only import what is needed.
I may select one, multiple, or all elements.
Clicking a checkbox must toggle selection state (checked → unchecked → checked).
clicking a checkbox to toggle selection
Selection state should persist while navigating backward from Step 3 to Step 2.
the system to preserve my selections even if I navigate back
I don’t have to reselect elements.
all my previous selections remain unchanged.
When user selects elements, counter updates live (e.g., “3 of 10 elements selected”).
I know how many elements I have selected.
When all elements are selected, counter shows “10 of 10 elements selected.”
the counter to reflect full selection
I know all elements have been selected.
The Cancel button must appear bottom-left of Step 2.
I can exit the import process if needed.
a Cancel button appears at the bottom-left.

60.title: Unsaved Changes Warning on Cancel or Navigation

content.text: The system shall display a confirmation popup when the user attempts to navigate away or cancel with unsaved changes present.
a warning popup before leaving Step 2
I don’t lose my selected elements accidentally.
I click Cancel or navigate away,
“You have unsaved changes. Do you want to leave without saving?”
The popup shall display a warning message indicating that unsaved changes will be lost.
a warning message indicating unsaved changes will be lost
I clearly understand the impact of cancelling.
the unsaved changes popup is open,
The popup shall contain a Continue Editing button that closes the popup and keeps the user on the same page.
a Continue Editing button in the popup
I can close the popup and keep working.
it includes a Continue Editing button.
I stay on the same page with no data lost.
The popup shall contain a Discard Changes button that removes unsaved data and exits the current editing flow.
I can exit the flow and clear unsaved selections.
it includes a Discard Changes button.
The modal shall prevent background interaction (greyed-out overlay). And The popup shall appear as a centered modal with the background dimmed.
the popup to prevent background interaction
I must make an explicit choice.
the background cannot be interacted with.
Clicking outside the popup shall not close it; the user must choose an action.
clicking outside the popup to do nothing
I cannot bypass the confirmation message.
Upon clicking Discard Changes, the system shall navigate the user back to the previous screen (e.g., Activity Phase list).
navigate back to the previous screen after discarding
I exit the import flow cleanly.
the system navigates back to the previous screen
No selections or progress shall be saved after cancellation.
I start fresh if I re-enter the flow.
none of the element selections or progress is saved.
The Next button shall appear at the bottom-right and shall be disabled until at least one element is selected.
the Next button disabled until at least one element is selected

69.title: Enable Next When Elements Are Selected

content.text: Once one or more elements are selected, the Next button must become active (blue).
the Next button active once I select an element
at least one element is selected,
Clicking Next shall validate selections and navigate to Step 3.
clicking Next to validate and proceed
at least one element is selected,

71.title: Step 2 Receives Library ID from Step 1

content.text: Step 2 shall receive the selected library ID from Step 1.
Step 2 to know which library is selected
the correct elements and mappings are shown.
Step 2 receives and uses the selected Library ID.
Selected element must be stored and passed to Step 3.
Step 3 can use them for preview and import.
the selected elements are passed forward.
Step 2 must preserve (saved data) user selections when navigating backward from Step 3.
my selections saved when I return to Step 2
I don’t lose my earlier choices.
the same selections are restored and highlighted.

74.title: Show Inline Error When No Elements Selected

content.text: If the user clicks Next with zero selections, an inline error must appear:
“Select at least one element to continue.”
an inline error if I try to continue with no selections
“Select at least one element to continue.”

75.title: Error When Element List Fails to Load

content.text: If element list fails to load, show message:
“Unable to load elements from the library. Please try again.”
a clear message if elements fail to load
“Unable to load elements from the library. Please try again.”
Stepper shall show Step 1 and Step 2 as completed with blue checkmarks.
the stepper to visually show progress
Step 3 shall be displayed as the active step (highlighted in blue).
I know it is the active step.
Subtitle shall show: “Import predefined activity phases and observation templates.”
Subtitle: “Import predefined activity phases and observation templates.”
A close (X) button shall remain available in the top-right corner.
I can exit the modal at any time.
a close (X) button appears in the top-right corner.
A section titled “Preview & Import” shall be shown at the top of Step 3.
a visible section title at the top of Step 3
I know this is the preview stage.

81.title: Display Selected Library Name in Step 3

content.text: The system shall display the name of the selected library (e.g., RAMS Library).
I know which library I am previewing.
the system displays the selected library name
The system shall show two summary counters:
see summary counters for Activity Phases and Observations
I understand what will be imported.
These counts must match data from the selected library.
the counts to reflect the selected library
the Activity Phase and Observation counts match the selected library’s data.
The ssystem shall display a table with the title “Applying to 10 elements”
a title above the preview table
I know how many elements are affected.
a table title appears: “Applying to 10 elements”
A table must list all selected elements chosen in Step 2.
the table to list all selected elements
Step 3 shows a table listing those elements.
the preview table to show required columns
I can compare warehouse names with library names.
Table rows shall not be editable in Step 3.
the table to be read-only in Step 3

88.title: Preview Updates When Returning to Step 2

content.text: If user navigates back to Step 2 and deselects elements, the preview table must update accordingly.
the preview table to update when elements change
returning to Step 3 updates the preview table accordingly.
A warning/info banner shall appear below the table.
an informational banner below the table
I understand the impact of importing.
an information banner appears below the preview table.
The notice text shall state: “This will add activity phases and observations to the selected elements. Existing data will not be overwritten.”
a clear explanation of the import behaviour
“This will add activity phases and observations to the selected elements. Existing data will not be overwritten.”
The banner must be non-dismissible to ensure user awareness.
it cannot be closed or dismissed by the user.
Cancel button must appear bottom-left inside the modal.
I can exit the import process.
a Cancel button appears at the bottom-left.
Clicking Cancel shall close the entire import workflow without saving any changes.
the Cancel button to exit the workflow
the system closes the full import process
I know it will apply the import.
the action button is labeled “Import & Apply”.
the Import & Apply button to import all phases and observations

96.title: Close Modal & Return to Activity Phase Page

content.text: Clicking Import & Apply shall close the modal and return the user to the Activity Phase page of the selected element.
return to the Activity Phase page after import
I can immediately see the results.
I return to the Activity Phase page for the selected element.
A success toast must appear to the top right corner: “Library imported successfully”.
I know the import completed correctly.
show a success toast at top-right:
After successful import, user shall be redirected automatically to the Activity Phase page of the element.
I continue the workflow without extra steps.
the system redirects automatically to the Activity Phase page of the selected element.
A green success notification shall appear in top-right: “Library imported successfully”. Of the activity phase screen.
it stands out on the Activity Phase screen.
the Activity Phase page loads after import,
Imported observation records shall be displayed in a table. Of that particular element.
imported observations to be shown immediately
all imported observations appear in an observations table

101.title: Apply Imports to All Selected Elements

content.text: All selected elements must receive the imported phases and observations uniformly.
all selected elements to receive the imported data
multiple elements were selected in Step 2,
If the import fails, the system must display a non-dismissible error banner.
I know the import did not succeed.
system displays a non-dismissible error banner.
User must not lose any existing observation data during import.
the import to protect existing observations
no historical or custom data is lost.
observations already exist for the element,
no existing records are deleted or overwritten

104.title: Success Toast When Import Triggered from Rules & Actions Screen

content.text: If the “Import from Library” option was clicked from the “Rules and Actions” main screen then the success toast message “Library imported successfully” will appear on that screen at the top right corner. and the user will be redirected to that screen.
the success toast to appear on the Rules & Actions screen
the feedback is consistent when import is initiated there.
Import from Library was launched from the Rules & Actions main screen,
show toast: “Library imported successfully” on that screen
redirect the user back to the Rules & Actions screen.
